growth_records.medium: glucose chemically defined medium (gCDM), open circuit

sec.method: calculated: 36.71 mM cumulative lactate at 38 h / 38 h = 0.966 mM/h (volumetric average, not biomass-specific)
calculated: 0.56 mM cumulative acetate at 38 h / 38 h = 0.0147 mM/h (volumetric average)

growth_records.cite: Leininger et al. Prophage induction and acetate availability are associated with distinct Lactiplantibacillus plantarum electrode responses. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00183-26. [PMC13185627]
growth_records.snippet: By 38 h, OC cultures produced 36.71 ± 2.02 mM lactate and 0.56 ± 0.02 mM acetate, whereas anodic cultures produced 9.31 ± 0.34 mM and 0.66 ± 0.04 mM, representing a more than fourfold increase in acetate: lactate production.
growth_records.notes: No numeric specific growth rate reported (growth curves in Fig 1A only); no biomass in gDW, so only volumetric average product rates derivable. Lactate rate = 36.71 mM / 38 h = 0.966 mM/h; acetate rate = 0.56 mM / 38 h = 0.0147 mM/h. Acetate:lactate molar yield = 0.56/36.71 = 0.0153. Glucose loading 10 g/L = 55.5 mM; glucose uptake shown in Fig 1B (no numeric value in text). Reactor temperature not stated (preculture was 37°C in MRS). pH: MOPS-buffered, NaOH added, no fixed setpoint stated for this run.

growth_records.medium: glucose chemically defined medium (gCDM), polarized anode

sec.method: calculated: 9.31 mM cumulative lactate at 38 h / 38 h = 0.245 mM/h (volumetric average)
calculated: 0.66 mM cumulative acetate at 38 h / 38 h = 0.0174 mM/h (volumetric average)
calculated: 181.2 C over 300 mL = 6.26 mM electron equivalent by 38 h; 6.26 mM / 38 h = 0.165 mM e-/h (181.2 C / 96485 C/mol = 1.878e-3 mol e- / 0.3 L = 6.26 mM)

growth_records.cite: Leininger et al. Prophage induction and acetate availability are associated with distinct Lactiplantibacillus plantarum electrode responses. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00183-26. [PMC13185627]
growth_records.snippet: By 38 h ... anodic cultures produced 9.31 ± 0.34 mM and 0.66 ± 0.04 mM ... By 38 h, an average of 181.2 coulombs of charge from the 300 mL medium volume had passed the anode corresponding to 6.26 mM electron equivalent.
growth_records.notes: Anode polarization slowed planktonic growth (Fig 1A, no numeric mu). Lactate rate = 9.31/38 = 0.245 mM/h; acetate rate = 0.66/38 = 0.0174 mM/h. Acetate:lactate = 0.66/9.31 = 0.0709, >4x the OC value (0.0153) as paper states. Anode electron flux = 181.2 C / 96485 C mol-1 = 1.878 mmol e- / 0.300 L = 6.26 mM e-, /38 h = 0.165 mM e-/h. Reactor temperature/pH setpoint not stated.

growth_records.medium: mannitol chemically defined medium (mCDM) + acetate, open circuit

up.method: acetate (8.47 mM initial) consumed with concomitant ethanol production; 819.3 ± 83.2 coulombs dissipated to acetate reduction by 75 h (4 mol e-/mol acetate to ethanol); time course of consumption not tabulated numerically

sec.method: ethanol increased concomitantly with acetate consumption (acetate-to-ethanol reduction), Fig 2D; no numeric endpoint in text
lactate accumulated as principal metabolite (Fig 2B); no numeric endpoint in text

growth_records.cite: Leininger et al. Prophage induction and acetate availability are associated with distinct Lactiplantibacillus plantarum electrode responses. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00183-26. [PMC13185627]
growth_records.snippet: By 65 h, OD600 reached 0.62 ± 0.05 in OC cultures vs 0.03 ± 0.01 under anodic conditions ... At 75 h, 819.3 ± 83.2 coulombs had been dissipated to acetate in the OC group.
growth_records.notes: OC mannitol+acetate: OD600 0.62 ± 0.05 at 65 h, plateaued at 0.66 ± 0.04 (65-164 h). Initial acetate 500 mg/L = 8.47 mM, consumed by OC with ethanol formed. Charge to acetate reduction 819.3 C at 75 h (4 e-/mol acetate to ethanol) => 819.3/96485 = 8.49 mmol e- / 0.300 L = 28.3 mM e-, /4 = 7.08 mM acetate reduced. No numeric mu; product time courses only in Fig 2. Temperature/pH setpoint not stated.

growth_records.medium: mannitol chemically defined medium (mCDM) + acetate, polarized anode

up.method: anode initially blocked acetate consumption; acetate increased to 8.64 ± 0.20 mM by 75 h, consumed only after population entered later phase

sec.method: 161.1 ± 38.9 coulombs dissipated to anode by 75 h in the acetate+ group (161.1/96485 = 1.67 mmol e-)

growth_records.cite: Leininger et al. Prophage induction and acetate availability are associated with distinct Lactiplantibacillus plantarum electrode responses. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00183-26. [PMC13185627]
growth_records.snippet: By 65 h, OD600 reached 0.62 ± 0.05 in OC cultures vs 0.03 ± 0.01 under anodic conditions (n = 3). Despite this delay, exponential growth rates were not significantly different (P = 0.47) ... The anode blocked acetate consumption initially,
growth_records.notes: Anodic mannitol+acetate: markedly prolonged lag (OD600 0.03 ± 0.01 at 65 h vs 0.62 OC) but exponential growth rate not significantly different from OC (P=0.47); continued to higher final OD than OC. Acetate retained/increased to 8.64 mM at 75 h (anode blocked acetate-to-ethanol). Charge to anode 161.1 C at 75 h = 161.1/96485 = 1.67 mmol e- / 0.300 L = 5.57 mM e-. No numeric mu reported. Temperature/pH setpoint not stated.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 6500 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/6500

phenotype_groups.label: Complex/peptone base (acid-production test)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)

phenotype_groups.label: Defined minimal medium (sole-substrate growth)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it

calls.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
calls.citation: BacDive strain 6499 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/6499

phenotype_groups.label: Enzyme/activity assay medium
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test

calls.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
